title: Create persistent tracker and enumerate remaining implementation steps
notes: Tracker created after user requested explicit sequential execution tracking.

title: Review current subagent resume implementation for gaps and define remaining concrete tasks to completion
notes: Identified remaining gaps: restored regular agents currently reopen as fresh sessions, restored fork-agent fidelity needs checking, resume-message trigger is overly broad, naming/docs cleanup remain.

title: Fix restore path fidelity issues so restored regular agents reopen exact child sessions rather than creating fresh sessions
notes: Regular agents now persist and restore via --session <childSessionFile> using resumeSessionFile, instead of reopening as fresh sessions in the shared session dir.

title: Fix restore path fidelity issues for restored fork agents and ensure persisted identity/session reuse semantics are correct
notes: Fork restore path already reopens exact child sessions via --session using resumeSessionFile. Removal replay keys off sessionId/sessionFile to distinguish reused short ids across incarnations.

title: Tighten generic resume-message behavior so it does not incorrectly fire for fresh persisted sessions if a better detectable signal exists in current architecture
notes: Kept session_start-based injection for --session restore coverage, but added guards to avoid reinjecting after an existing resume marker and to skip likely fresh fork starts whose last user message is the synthetic Task: prompt.

title: Refactor remaining subagent runtime/module naming away from group-centric terminology where it affects current architecture clarity
notes: Renamed runtime module file from group.ts to agent-set.ts and updated imports/usages. Kept <group_complete> XML for compatibility, but runtime code no longer centers the concept.

title: Update codemap and relevant docs/prompts to reflect persistent per-parent subagent storage, append-only lifecycle logging, and resume behavior
notes: Updated codemap, onboard prompt, and orchestrating-agents skill to describe persistent per-parent child sessions, append-only lifecycle logging, resume/replay, semantic teardown, and later split standalone resume semantics into a separate extension module.

title: Run targeted code inspection sanity pass for the changed subagent files and resolve any obvious consistency issues
notes: Resolved obvious consistency issues found during inspection: regular restore now uses --session, agent-set module rename propagated, restore hook now keys off manager.hasAgents instead of unused ack string, and removed broken fresh-fork heuristic from resume-message logic.
